$description: cenno design tokens — W3C DTCG format. Source of truth for src/styles/tokens.css (built by Style Dictionary, see style-dictionary.config.mjs). Validated by scripts/validate-tokens.mjs (npm run validate:tokens). Format notes: dimensions use the string form ("44px") because style-dictionary 4.x does not yet build the newer value+unit object draft form; letter-spacing uses em (a documented deviation — the DTCG dimension unit enum is px|rem only, but tracking must scale with font size).

color.$description: Reporter palette — one saturated hue per flow, white ink on top.

flow.$description: Flow surface hues. One hue per flow; components consume these only via the semantic theme vars in src/styles/theme.css.

type.$description: Font-size scale (SF Pro via the system stack, see font.family).

question.$description: Question display sizes — the question is the only chrome.

font.$description: Typographic solution — faces, weights, tracking and leading. Sizes live in the type group.

default.$description: System stack — SF Pro on macOS. One face for everything; hierarchy comes from size, weight and color, never from a second family.

caption.$description: Regular — captions stay quiet; tracking does the work

default.$description: Questions and body — SF Pro needs no help

caption.$description: Uppercase captions, metadata, the wordmark — loose tracking at small sizes. NOTE: em is outside the DTCG dimension unit enum (px|rem); kept deliberately so tracking scales with font size.

space.$description: Spacing scale — 8 16 24 40 64. Tap targets stay >= 44px via padding, never font inflation.

brand.$description: cenno brand metadata — the mark and its geometry. Non-token data lives in $extensions per the DTCG spec; this group emits no CSS variables. See docs/design/BRAND.md.

app.cenno.mark.name: arc-over-dot
app.cenno.mark.meaning: Un cenno — the small sign. An arc beckoning over a dot: the app summons, the person answers.
app.cenno.mark.usage: Template/monochrome (currentColor or black-on-transparent) in system contexts — tray, menus. May be drawn in any color.flow hue in brand contexts. Never multi-color, never outlined dot.
